description: Logical and physical access security. The entity implements logical access security software, infrastructure, and architectures over protected information assets to protect them from security events to meet the entity's objectives. Covers role-based access control, least privilege, access provisioning and revocation.

subcriteria.name: rbac_definition
subcriteria.reference_text: Role-based access control with defined permission tiers, user roles, and authorization matrix. Each role has specific access levels and permissions to systems and data.

subcriteria.name: least_privilege
subcriteria.reference_text: Least privilege principle requiring users have minimum necessary access. Need-to-know basis for data access. Access restricted to job function requirements.

subcriteria.name: access_provisioning
subcriteria.reference_text: Access provisioning and request process. Formal workflow for requesting, approving, and granting system access. Manager approval required before access is granted.

subcriteria.name: access_revocation
subcriteria.reference_text: Access revocation and termination procedures. Timely removal of access when employees leave or change roles. Deprovisioning checklist and confirmation process.

subcriteria.name: periodic_access_review
subcriteria.reference_text: Periodic access reviews conducted quarterly or annually. Manager certification of access rights. Review logs showing approval dates and approvers.

mismatch_signals.reason: Document describes physical (badge/keycard) access controls, not logical system access controls.
Document appears to be about network access (CC6.6) rather than logical access control (CC6.1).
HR benefits document, not an access control policy.
